meta.description: Vura AI Global Exams Database — Subjects, Scoring, and Details

exams.exam_full_name: Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination
exams.subject_note: 1 mandatory (English) + 3 optional based on course of study

score_ranges.description: Barely qualifies for some state universities
Eligible for polytechnics and some lower-tier universities
Eligible for most universities; competitive for some courses
Competitive for popular courses at reputable universities
Strong chance at top federal universities (UNILAG, UI, OAU)
Top 1% — eligible for Medicine, Law, Engineering at any university

tips: English is mandatory — strong vocabulary and comprehension are essential
Practice past questions from 2000 to present
Time management: 120 minutes for 180 questions = 40 seconds per question
CBT format — practice on a computer before exam day

exams.exam_full_name: West African Examinations Council Senior School Certificate Examination
exams.subject_note: English and Maths are compulsory; remaining subjects are electives based on track

score_ranges.label: A1 (75–100%)
score_ranges.description: Exceptional — top universities worldwide will accept this

score_ranges.label: B2-B3 (65–74%)
score_ranges.description: Strong performance — qualifies for competitive university courses

score_ranges.label: C4-C6 (50–64%)
score_ranges.description: Minimum university entry standard; 5 credits including English & Maths required

score_ranges.label: D7-E8 (40–49%)
score_ranges.description: Does not meet credit standard; resit recommended for university entry

score_ranges.label: F9 (0–39%)
score_ranges.description: Must resit — this grade is unacceptable for further education

exams.university_requirement: Minimum 5 credits including English Language and Mathematics
tips: WAEC requires both objective and theory sections — practice both thoroughly
5 credits minimum (including English + Maths) required for university admission
Biology, Chemistry, Physics practicals are separately graded
Past questions from 1990 onwards are available on Vura AI

exams.exam_full_name: National Examinations Council Senior School Certificate Examination
exams.subject_note: English and Maths are compulsory; NECO is conducted only in Nigeria (unlike WAEC which covers West Africa)

score_ranges.label: A1 (75–100%)
score_ranges.description: Outstanding — accepted by all Nigerian universities

score_ranges.label: B2-B3 (65–74%)
score_ranges.description: Strong result — qualifies for competitive programmes

score_ranges.label: C4-C6 (50–64%)
score_ranges.description: Meets university admission requirement when combined with other credits

score_ranges.label: D7-E8 (40–49%)
score_ranges.description: Below credit standard — resit needed for university

score_ranges.label: F9 (0–39%)
score_ranges.description: Must resit — fails to meet any tertiary admission standard

exams.university_requirement: Minimum 5 credits including English Language and Mathematics
tips: NECO questions are often more theoretical than WAEC — master definitions and explanations
NECO and WAEC results are equally accepted by Nigerian universities
Use Vura AI to practice both WAEC and NECO past questions interchangeably
NECO internal (school-based) and external (national) exams are different — this covers the national exam

exams.exam_full_name: West African Senior School Certificate Examination (Ghana)
exams.subject_note: Ghana WASSCE has 4 mandatory core subjects unlike Nigeria WAEC which has 2 core subjects

score_ranges.label: A1 (80–100%)
score_ranges.description: Top performance — qualifies for all Ghanaian universities

score_ranges.label: B2-B3 (60–79%)
score_ranges.description: Strong — eligible for competitive programmes at UG, KNUST, UCC

score_ranges.label: C4-C6 (45–59%)
score_ranges.description: Minimum for university — 6 credits including core subjects required

score_ranges.label: D7-E8 (35–44%)
score_ranges.description: Below credit standard — resit needed for higher education

score_ranges.label: F9 (0–34%)
score_ranges.description: Must resit — does not qualify for tertiary admission

tips: Core subjects (English, Maths, Integrated Science, Social Studies) are mandatory for all students
Elective Mathematics is different from Core Mathematics — it's more advanced
KNUST and UG require specific elective combinations for Science/Business programmes
Practice WASSCE past questions from 2005 onwards on Vura AI

exams.exam_full_name: Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education
exams.subject_note: English and Kiswahili are mandatory; students select remaining subjects across groups

score_ranges.label: A / A- (Mean 10–12)
score_ranges.description: Top performance — qualifies for Medicine, Engineering, Law at University of Nairobi, Strathmore

score_ranges.label: B+ / B / B- (Mean 7–9)
score_ranges.description: Strong result — eligible for most degree programmes at Kenyan public universities

score_ranges.label: C+ / C (Mean 5–6)
score_ranges.description: Minimum for government-sponsored degree placement; qualifies for diploma/certificate if C+

score_ranges.label: C- / D+ (Mean 3–4)
score_ranges.description: Qualifies for certificate and diploma programmes; artisan courses

score_ranges.label: D / D- / E (Mean 1–2)
score_ranges.description: Does not qualify for certificate or diploma programmes

exams.university_requirement: Minimum grade C+ (Mean Grade 7+) for government-sponsored degree placement
tips: Mean grade is calculated across all subjects — a strong performance in all subjects matters
C+ is the critical threshold for degree programme placement in Kenya
Mathematics and Sciences are heavily weighted for Science/Technology courses
Use Vura AI's past KCSE questions from 2010 to 2024 for intensive practice

exams.exam_full_name: National Senior Certificate (South African Matriculation Examination)
exams.subject_count: Minimum 6 subjects (must include Home Language, one additional language, and Life Orientation)
exams.subject_note: Life Orientation and two language subjects are compulsory; Mathematics OR Mathematical Literacy is required

pass_levels.higher_certificate_pass: 40% in 3 subjects + 30% in 3 others (including Home Language)
pass_levels.bachelor_pass: 50% in Home Language + 50% in 4 designated subjects + 30% in other subjects

score_ranges.label: Level 7 (80–100%)
score_ranges.description: Top tier — qualifies for Medicine, Engineering, Law at UCT, Wits, Stellenbosch

score_ranges.label: Level 6 (70–79%)
score_ranges.description: Excellent — Bachelor's degree admission at most South African universities

score_ranges.label: Level 5 (60–69%)
score_ranges.description: Good — Qualifies for Bachelor's degree; competitive for popular courses

score_ranges.label: Level 4 (50–59%)
score_ranges.description: Meets Bachelor's pass requirement in most subjects

score_ranges.label: Level 3 (40–49%)
score_ranges.description: Meets Diploma pass; does not qualify for Bachelor's degree entry

score_ranges.label: Level 2 (30–39%)
score_ranges.description: Higher Certificate pass only; limited tertiary options

score_ranges.label: Level 1 (0–29%)
score_ranges.description: Failed — must repeat or supplement the subject

tips: Bachelor's pass requires 50% in 4 designated subjects — focus on these
Mathematics is critical for university admission to Science, Engineering, and Commerce
Mathematical Literacy is easier but limits your university course options
School-Based Assessment (SBA) contributes 25% to your final mark — do not neglect class work

exams.exam_full_name: National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (Undergraduate) — Medical

score_ranges.description: AIIMS Delhi, JIPMER — top government medical colleges assured
Strong government medical college in top states (Maharashtra, Karnataka, UP)
Government medical college in smaller states; private medical college options
Private medical colleges (management quota); some state colleges
Deemed universities; very limited government options
Qualifies for admission but seats very limited; consider repeating

score_ranges.label: Below 138
score_ranges.description: Does not qualify for MBBS/BDS admission — must retake

eligibility.class_12_requirement: PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) with minimum 50% aggregate

tips: NCERT textbooks are the bible for NEET — master every line
Biology carries 360/720 marks — top priority subject
Negative marking (-1 per wrong answer) — do not guess blindly
Solve at least 10 years of past NEET papers before the exam

exams.exam_full_name: Joint Entrance Examination Main — Engineering

question_types: Numerical Answer Type (NAT) — 10 per subject (5 to attempt)

score_ranges.label: 250–300 (Percentile 99.9+)
score_ranges.description: Top NITs (Trichy, Warangal, Surathkal) — Core Engineering branches

score_ranges.label: 200–249 (Percentile 99–99.9)
score_ranges.description: Good NITs, IIITs, and top state universities

score_ranges.label: 150–199 (Percentile 95–99)
score_ranges.description: Mid-tier NITs and IIITs; qualifies for JEE Advanced

score_ranges.label: 100–149 (Percentile 85–95)
score_ranges.description: State government engineering colleges; some private top-tier colleges

score_ranges.label: Below 89
score_ranges.description: Below cut-off for NITs; state colleges only based on board results

eligibility.class_12_requirement: PCM (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) — passing required; 75% for NITs/IIITs/GFTIs

tips: JEE Main qualifies you for NITs, IIITs, and GFTIs — and is the gateway to JEE Advanced (IITs)
Top 2.5 lakh students in JEE Main are eligible to appear for JEE Advanced
Take both January and April sessions to improve your best percentile
Focus on NCERT for Chemistry; use HC Verma for Physics; RD Sharma or Cengage for Maths

exams.exam_full_name: Joint Entrance Examination Advanced — IIT Entrance
exams.exam_body: IITs (Rotating Organiser — IIT Bombay, Delhi, Madras, etc.)
exams.exam_level: Post-Secondary — Most Competitive Engineering Exam in India
exams.marking_scheme: Varies by question type — partial marking exists for Multiple Correct MCQs; full negative marking applies

score_ranges.description: IIT Bombay / Delhi / Madras — CS, EE, Mechanical top branches assured
Old IITs (Kanpur, Kharagpur, Roorkee) — core branches
New IITs — most branches available
Newer IITs — less popular branches

score_ranges.label: Below qualifying cut-off
score_ranges.description: Does not qualify for IIT admission; JoSAA counselling not available

eligibility.jee_main_requirement: Must be in top 2.5 lakh qualifiers of JEE Main
eligibility.class_12_requirement: Must have appeared in Class 12 in the same or previous year

tips: JEE Advanced is the only gateway to 23 IITs in India
Conceptual depth is more important than memorisation
Practice papers from 2000–2024 extensively — question patterns repeat themes
Manage both papers on exam day — Paper 1 and Paper 2 are held on the same day

exams.exam_full_name: National College Entrance Examination (全国统一高考)
exams.exam_body: Ministry of Education (MOE) — Provincial Education Departments
exams.subject_note: Gaokao format varies by province — some provinces use traditional Science/Arts track, others use 3+3 or 3+1+2 reform systems

score_ranges.description: Peking University (北大), Tsinghua University (清华) — any major
985 Project Universities (Fudan, Zhejiang, SJTU, USTC)
211 Project Universities — strong departments
First-tier provincial universities (本科一批)
Second-tier universities (本科二批)

score_ranges.label: Below 400
score_ranges.description: Vocational colleges (专科/高职) — university admission very limited

tips: The Gaokao is held on the exact same date nationwide (June 7–8) — a national event
Your province's cut-off scores differ — research your specific province's 分数线
English listening test is usually conducted before the main exam in some provinces
Start serious preparation in Grade 10 — three years of consistent study is standard

exams.exam_full_name: College Scholastic Ability Test (대학수학능력시험 / Suneung)
exams.exam_body: Korea Institute for Curriculum and Evaluation (KICE)
exams.subject_count: 4 mandatory + 2 from Social/Science/Vocational + optional 2nd Foreign Language

Grade 1.description: Exceptional — Seoul National University (SNU), Yonsei, Korea University (SKY)

score_ranges.label: Grade 1 (Top 4%)
score_ranges.description: Seoul National, Yonsei, Korea University — all departments available

score_ranges.label: Grade 2 (4–11%)
score_ranges.description: Top 10 universities — Medicine, Engineering, Business at top schools

score_ranges.label: Grade 3 (11–23%)
score_ranges.description: Top 20 universities — broad range of competitive programmes

score_ranges.label: Grade 4–5 (23–60%)
score_ranges.description: Regional universities and colleges

score_ranges.label: Grade 6–9 (Bottom 40%)
score_ranges.description: Community colleges; resit recommended

tips: Suneung is held on one day only — extreme stress and preparation across Korea
EBS (국가교육방송) past papers are directly linked to Gaokao questions — study them thoroughly
Entire country goes quiet on exam day — planes are grounded, businesses open late
English Grade 1 requires near-perfect score — practise with native-level materials

exams.exam_full_name: Common Test for University Admissions (大学入学共通テスト / Daigaku Nyūgaku Kyōtsū Test)
exams.exam_body: National Center for University Entrance Examinations (NCUEE / 大学入試センター)

group_3_mathematics: Mathematics II / Mathematics II & B / Mathematics C
group_6_information: Information I (情報 — newly added from 2025)

exams.subject_note: Most national universities require 5 subjects minimum; top universities require 7–8; private universities may require fewer

score_ranges.description: University of Tokyo (東大), Kyoto University (京大) — all faculties
Osaka, Tohoku, Nagoya, Hokkaido — top national universities
Strong national and public universities
Regional national universities; private universities

score_ranges.label: Below 500
score_ranges.description: Private university with lower cut-offs; junior colleges

tips: The Common Test replaced the Centre Test (センター試験) in 2021 — focus on new format
English now has no grammar-only questions — all reading and listening comprehension
Most top universities (Tokyo, Kyoto) require the Common Test PLUS their own secondary exam
Information (情報) became mandatory in 2025 — do not skip this subject

exams.exam_full_name: Group-based Standardised Test for Public University Admission (Bangladesh)
exams.subject_count: Varies by unit (4–6 subjects per unit)
exams.subject_note: Students apply for a specific unit based on their HSC track (Science, Arts, Commerce); some universities conduct their own separate tests

score_ranges.description: Dhaka University, BUET, RUET — top programmes
Rajshahi University, Chittagong University — competitive programmes
Regional public universities — most programmes available
Qualifies for some public universities; limited programme options

score_ranges.label: Below 40
score_ranges.description: Does not qualify for public university admission

tips: HSC results contribute to the final merit score — high grades help significantly
BUET (Bangladesh Engineering) has its own separate admission test — prepare specifically
Medical admission (MBBS) in Bangladesh uses a separate test — not covered under GST
Negative marking (-0.25) — avoid random guessing

exams.exam_full_name: Yükseköğretim Kurumları Sınavı (Higher Education Institutions Examination)
exams.exam_body: Student Selection and Placement Centre (ÖSYM — Ölçme, Seçme ve Yerleştirme Merkezi)
exams.max_score: Score Type-dependent (AYT max approx. 500 per section)

TYT_session.name: Temel Yeterlilik Testi (TYT) — Basic Proficiency Test
TYT_session.min_score_for_AYT: TYT score ≥ 150 points required to take AYT

AYT_session.name: Alan Yeterlilik Testi (AYT) — Field Proficiency Test

score_ranges.label: 480–500 (SAY)
score_ranges.description: Boğaziçi University, ODTÜ (METU), İTÜ — Engineering, Medicine, Law
Top 5 Turkish universities — competitive programmes
Top 20 universities — most departments available
State universities in major cities — many departments
Regional state universities; foundation universities (vakıf üniversitesi)

score_ranges.label: Below 150 TYT
score_ranges.description: Does not qualify to take AYT; cannot place into 4-year programmes

tips: TYT is the prerequisite — score ≥150 to qualify for AYT and university placement
Boğaziçi and ODTÜ require near-perfect AYT scores in Science track
English language track (Dil) gives access to English-medium universities
Previous YGS/LYS exam papers are still useful for practice as question styles are similar

exams.exam_full_name: Medical and Dental College Admission Test (Pakistan)
exams.exam_body: Pakistan Medical & Dental Council (PMDC) / National Testing Service (NTS) / Provincial PMCs

merit_formula.note: Final merit = weighted combination of Matric, FSc, and MDCAT marks

score_ranges.description: King Edward Medical University (KEMU), Allama Iqbal Medical College, Dow Medical
Top provincial government medical colleges
Government medical colleges — depends on province and merit list
Borderline government colleges; private medical colleges

score_ranges.label: Below 110
score_ranges.description: Does not meet minimum threshold for medical admission

eligibility.education: FSc Pre-Medical (Biology, Chemistry, Physics) with minimum 60% marks
eligibility.age: 17 years minimum on 31st December of admission year

tips: Biology is the highest-weightage subject (34%) — master it thoroughly
FSc marks contribute 40% to your final merit — do not neglect board exams
Negative marking (-0.25) applies — strategic answering is important
KEMU (King Edward) is the most prestigious — requires MDCAT 190+

exams.exam_full_name: Engineering College Admission Test (Pakistan)
exams.exam_body: University of Engineering & Technology (UET) — Provincial Engineering Universities

score_ranges.description: UET Lahore — Civil, Electrical, Mechanical (top departments)
UET Taxila, NUST — strong departments
NED University, MUET — competitive departments
Regional engineering universities; private colleges

score_ranges.label: Below 160
score_ranges.description: Private engineering colleges only; government options very limited

tips: Mathematics and Physics together carry 60% of marks — prioritise them
UET Lahore is the most competitive — requires ECAT 350+ for top departments
FSc marks carry 40% weight in merit — high intermediate grades are crucial
Negative marking applies — do not randomly guess on unknown questions

exams.exam_full_name: Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (Malaysian Certificate of Education)
exams.exam_body: Malaysian Examinations Syndicate (Lembaga Peperiksaan Malaysia / LPM)
exams.exam_format: Written (Objective + Essay + Practical for science subjects)

core_compulsory: Islamic Education (for Muslims) / Moral Education (for non-Muslims)

exams.subject_note: Bahasa Melayu is mandatory and must be passed; a credit in BM is required for public university admission

score_ranges.label: A+ / A (80–100%)
score_ranges.description: Universiti Malaya (UM), USM, UKM — competitive programmes

score_ranges.label: A- / B+ (65–79%)
score_ranges.description: Public universities (UITM, UPM) — most programmes

score_ranges.label: B / C+ (55–64%)
score_ranges.description: Matriculation/Foundation programmes; private universities

score_ranges.label: C / D (40–54%)
score_ranges.description: Certificate programmes; some private colleges

score_ranges.label: E / G (Below 40%)
score_ranges.description: Must resit; does not qualify for tertiary admission in that subject

tips: Credit in Bahasa Melayu is mandatory for public university admission — never neglect it
Additional Mathematics (Add Maths) is critical for Science/Engineering university entry
SPM results are released approximately 4 months after exams — March the following year
5 As is a strong benchmark for scholarship applications to government bodies like JPA, MARA

exams.exam_full_name: Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (Malaysian Higher School Certificate)
exams.exam_body: Malaysian Examinations Council (Majlis Peperiksaan Malaysia / MPM)

score_ranges.label: CGPA 3.67–4.00 (A- to A)
score_ranges.description: Universiti Malaya (UM), USM, UKM — Medicine, Engineering, Law

score_ranges.label: CGPA 3.00–3.67 (B to B+)
score_ranges.description: Public universities — most competitive programmes

score_ranges.label: CGPA 2.00–3.00 (C to B-)
score_ranges.description: Public universities — less competitive programmes; private universities

score_ranges.label: CGPA 1.00–2.00 (D- to C-)
score_ranges.description: Private universities and colleges; some foundation programmes

score_ranges.label: CGPA Below 1.00
score_ranges.description: Must repeat; does not qualify for degree admission

tips: STPM is equivalent to UK A-Levels — one of the hardest pre-university exams in Asia
3 terms of School-Based Assessment (SBA) contribute to the final grade
Pengajian Am (General Studies) is compulsory for all STPM students
STPM A grades are highly competitive for UM, USM scholarships

exams.exam_full_name: General Certificate of Secondary Education
exams.exam_duration_minutes: Varies per subject (1.5–2.5 hours per paper)
exams.subject_note: English Language, English Literature, and Maths are almost universally required; Science is compulsory

9.description: Top Grade — above A* (awarded to top ~3% in each subject)

5.description: Strong C / Low B — Strong Pass

4.description: Standard Pass — equivalent to old grade C

score_ranges.label: Grade 9 (Top ~3%)
score_ranges.description: Top-performing students — Oxbridge, Russell Group universities assured with A-Levels

score_ranges.label: Grade 7–8 (A/A*)
score_ranges.description: Strong foundation for A-Levels; needed for competitive sixth forms

score_ranges.label: Grade 5–6 (Strong Pass)
score_ranges.description: Accepted by most sixth forms and colleges for A-Level study

score_ranges.label: Grade 4 (Standard Pass)
score_ranges.description: Minimum for most employment and further education; retake English/Maths if below 4

score_ranges.label: Grade 1–3
score_ranges.description: Below employer and FE college threshold — retake advised

score_ranges.label: Grade U
score_ranges.description: Must resit — no qualification awarded

exams.english_maths_requirement: Grade 4 in English Language and Mathematics must be retaken if not achieved by age 16

exam_dates.results: August (Results Day — last Thursday of August)

tips: Focus on English Language, English Literature, and Mathematics — these are gateways to everything
Grade 4 is the minimum; grade 5 is the 'strong pass' employers and universities prefer
EBacc subjects (English, Maths, Science, Language, History/Geography) significantly improve university prospects
Past papers from each exam board (AQA, Edexcel, OCR) differ — practise specifically for your board

exams.exam_full_name: General Certificate of Education Advanced Level
exams.exam_body: AQA / Edexcel (Pearson) / OCR / Cambridge International (CIE) / WJEC
exams.exam_duration_minutes: Varies per subject (1.5–3 hours per paper)
exams.subject_count: Typically 3 A-Levels (4 possible in Year 12 — AS Level)
exams.subject_note: Students choose 3 A-Level subjects; subject choice determines university course eligibility (e.g., Medicine requires Chemistry + Biology)

A*.description: Outstanding — above 90% UMS overall with 90%+ in A2 units

score_ranges.label: A*A*A* (168 UCAS points)
score_ranges.description: Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial — Medicine, Law, Mathematics, Computer Science

score_ranges.label: A*AA / AAA (152–160 points)
score_ranges.description: Top Russell Group (UCL, Edinburgh, LSE, King's College)

score_ranges.label: AAB / ABB (136–144 points)
score_ranges.description: Strong Russell Group and redbrick universities

score_ranges.label: BBB / BBC (112–120 points)
score_ranges.description: Most UK universities — broad programme access

score_ranges.label: BCC / CCC (96–104 points)
score_ranges.description: Post-92 universities; some less competitive programmes

score_ranges.label: DDE / EEE (48–72 points)
score_ranges.description: Foundation programmes; some colleges — limited options

score_ranges.label: U (0 points)
score_ranges.description: No UCAS points; does not qualify for university admission

exam_dates.results: Mid-August (Results Day — 2 days before GCSE results)

tips: Choose A-Level subjects aligned to your university course — Medicine needs Chemistry, Law benefits from History/English
A* in Mathematics and Further Mathematics is the benchmark for top Engineering and CS programmes
Past papers from your specific exam board (AQA vs Edexcel vs OCR) — questions and mark schemes differ
Cambridge A-Levels (CIE) are recognised internationally in 160+ countries — the global gold standard

exams.exam_full_name: Scholastic Assessment Test
exams.subject_note: Digital SAT (from 2024) replaced the paper SAT; adaptive testing — second module difficulty adjusts based on first module performance

score_ranges.description: Harvard, MIT, Stanford, Ivy League — all schools within reach
Elite universities — strong candidate for highly selective schools
Selective universities — UC Berkeley, UCLA, Carnegie Mellon
Many strong universities — broad acceptance range
Regional universities and state schools

score_ranges.label: Below 900
score_ranges.description: Community colleges; retake highly recommended for 4-year universities

test_dates.sessions: 6 times per year (March, May, June, August, October, November)
test_dates.international_dates: 5 times per year (March, May, August, October, December)

tips: Digital SAT (2024+) is adaptive — perform well in Module 1 to get harder (higher-scoring) Module 2
Most colleges are now test-optional but a strong SAT score (1400+) still strengthens applications
Reading & Writing tests reading comprehension — practice reading academic passages daily
Desmos graphing calculator is built into the digital SAT — learn to use it efficiently

exams.exam_full_name: American College Testing
exams.exam_format: Written (Paper) / Digital (Pilot) — Multiple Choice + Optional Essay

Mathematics.calculator_note: Scientific or graphing calculator permitted throughout

Science.note: Science is NOT biology/chemistry — it tests scientific reasoning from data

content: One argumentative essay prompt — analyse multiple perspectives on a contemporary issue

exams.composite_score_calculation: Average of English + Mathematics + Reading + Science scores

score_ranges.description: All Ivy League and elite universities; exceptional candidate
Highly selective universities — Stanford, MIT, Johns Hopkins
Selective universities — strong application component
Many reputable universities — within average for competitive programmes
Regional and state universities — acceptable range

score_ranges.label: Below 17
score_ranges.description: Community colleges; strongly consider retaking for 4-year universities

test_dates.sessions: 7 times per year (February, April, June, July, September, October, December)

tips: ACT Science is NOT memorised science — it is data interpretation. Practice reading graphs and experimental setups
ACT Math includes trigonometry (unlike SAT) — ensure you cover all trig topics
Take both SAT and ACT to see which fits your strengths — submit the better score
30+ ACT or 1360+ SAT is the benchmark for selective university admissions
The optional Writing essay is recommended if applying to highly selective colleges
